message.content:
Research the current state (May 2026) of @tailwindcss/typography compatibility with Tailwind CSS v4. I need to know:

1. Does @tailwindcss/typography work with Tailwind v4 (which uses @tailwindcss/vite, no tailwind.config.js)?
2. Is there a v4-compatible version of the typography plugin? What's the install command and how is it configured (in CSS, not a config file)?
3. If it doesn't work, what's the recommended approach for prose/article styling in Tailwind v4?

Search npm for @tailwindcss/typography latest version and check its README or changelog for v4 support. Also check the Tailwind v4 docs for typography/prose patterns.

Report concisely: does it work, how to install, how to configure in a CSS file (not tailwind.config.js since v4 dropped that).

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.

- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding @tailwindcss/typography compatibility with Tailwind CSS v4:

## Tailwind CSS v4 Compatibility

The plugin added explicit support for Tailwind v4 in recent releases. If you upgraded Tailwind, update @tailwindcss/typography too.

## How to Use with v4

For Tailwind CSS v4, add the plugin to your main style.css file using `@import "tailwindcss";` followed by `@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";`

## Configuration in v4

If you need to change the generated CSS itself, enable JS config in v4 with @config, then extend the typography theme object in your tailwind.config.js file. You can also change the class name when registering the plugin in v4 using `@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography" { className: wysiwyg; }`

## Known Issues

There have been some compatibility issues reported. Some users updated to v4 and found that Typography is simply not being loaded. However, ensuring you added `@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";` in your CSS and updated the plugin to a v4-compatible release should resolve these issues.

Based on the search results, here's information about installing and configuring @tailwindcss/typography v4:

## Installation

Install the plugin from npm: npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography

## Configuration for Tailwind CSS v4

Add the plugin to your main style.css file: @import "tailwindcss"; + @plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";

## Configuration for Tailwind CSS v3

If you are still using Tailwind CSS v3, add the plugin to your tailwind.config.js file: module.exports = { theme: { // ... }, plugins: [ require('@tailwindcss/typography'), // ... ], }

## Basic Usage

Now you can use the prose classes to add sensible typography styles to any vanilla HTML: <article class="prose lg:prose-xl">

## Custom Class Names (v4)

You can customize the class name using @plugin "@tailwindcss/typography" { className: wysiwyg; }

## Advanced Customization

If you need to change the generated CSS itself, enable JS config in v4 with @config, then extend the typography theme object in a tailwind.config.js file

The main difference between v3 and v4 is that v4 uses a CSS-first configuration approach where you import plugins directly in your CSS file, rather than using a JavaScript configuration file.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tailwind CSS v4 prose styling and typography plugins:

## Official Typography Plugin

The official Tailwind CSS Typography plugin provides a set of prose classes you can use to add beautiful typographic defaults to any vanilla HTML you don't control, like HTML rendered from Markdown, or pulled from a CMS.

### For Tailwind CSS v4

You can add the typography plugin in your CSS with `@import "tailwindcss"` and `@plugin '@tailwindcss/typography'`. The plugin added explicit support for Tailwind v4 in recent releases.

### Features

The Tailwind Typography plugin is a first-party plugin that transforms raw, unstyled HTML content into clean, well-formatted typography using a single utility prose class, applying a well-balanced set of typographic defaults to the child elements of any container it's applied to, therefore automatically styling elements like paragraphs, headings, lists, blockquotes, and tables to look polished and readable right out of the box.

### Alternative: CSS-Only Implementation

tw-prose is a CSS-only implementation of the Tailwind Typography plugin built specifically for Tailwind CSS v4, offering elegant, consistent text styling without any plugin overhead.

### Customization

To change the generated CSS itself in v4, enable JS config with @config, then extend the typography theme object in your tailwind.config.js file.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.content:
The server returned HTTP 403 Forbidden.

The response body was not retrieved. If this URL requires authentication, use an authenticated tool (e.g. `gh` for GitHub, or an MCP-provided fetch tool) instead of WebFetch.

# Tailwind CSS Typography Plugin Overview

The Tailwind CSS Typography plugin provides pre-built styling classes for unstyled HTML content. Here are the key details:

## Purpose
This official plugin delivers "beautiful typographic defaults for HTML you don't control," making it ideal for rendering markdown, CMS content, and similar sources.

## Core Features

**Basic Implementation:**
Apply the `prose` class to any container element to instantly style its text content with sensible typography.

**Gray Scale Options:**
Five color themes are available—gray, slate, zinc, neutral, and stone—each corresponding to Tailwind's default palettes.

**Size Modifiers:**
Five responsive type scales exist: `prose-sm`, `prose-base`, `prose-lg`, `prose-xl`, and `prose-2xl`, enabling size adjustments across different screen sizes.

**Dark Mode Support:**
The `prose-invert` class provides hand-designed dark variants for each color theme.

## Advanced Customization

**Element Modifiers:**
Target specific HTML elements directly using modifiers like `prose-headings:underline` or `prose-a:text-blue-600`.

**Width Control:**
The `max-w-none` utility overrides default max-widths when full-width content is needed.

**Exclusion:**
The `not-prose` class prevents styling from applying to nested elements.

## Installation & Configuration

For Tailwind v4, add the plugin via npm and register it in your stylesheet. Earlier versions require configuration in `tailwind.config.js`.

The plugin supports customization through CSS variables, JavaScript configuration, and custom class names via the `className` option.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about @tailwindcss/typography with Tailwind v4:

## Latest Version

The latest version of @tailwindcss/typography is 0.5.19, last published 7 months ago.

## Compatibility with Tailwind v4

The @tailwindcss/typography plugin does work with Tailwind v4, though the configuration approach has changed. For Tailwind v4, you import the plugin using `@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography"` at the top of your CSS file, and can then create customizations by adding a separate tailwind.config.js file with the typography section.

## Important Notes

When setting custom --tw-prose-* variables in @theme for both light and dark modes, adding dark:prose-invert will override your custom values with Typography's defaults in dark mode. Only use dark:prose-invert if you're relying entirely on the Typography plugin's built-in dark palette.

The search results indicate that there are plans to revisit the typography plugin at some point, with discussions about improving the customization experience. However, the current stable version (0.5.19) continues to be maintained and is compatible with Tailwind v4.

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about configuring the `@tailwindcss/typography` plugin with Tailwind CSS v4 using the `@plugin` directive:

## Basic Configuration for Tailwind v4

In Tailwind CSS v4, you should add the plugin to your main style.css file using: `@import "tailwindcss"; @plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";`

## Key CSS-First Approach Changes

In Tailwind v4, `@plugin` replaces the plugins array, and you no longer need a separate config file for most projects.

## Complete Configuration Example

A basic v4 CSS configuration looks like: `@import "tailwindcss"; @plugin "@tailwindcss/typography"; @theme { --color-accent: oklch(0.75 0.18 175); --font-family-heading: "JetBrains Mono", monospace; }`

## Customization with Config File

If you need to customize the typography styles, you can use the @config directive: `@import "tailwindcss"; @plugin "@tailwindcss/typography"; @config "./tailwind.config.js";` Then configure the typography theme in your `tailwind.config.js` file.

## Custom Plugin Options

You can customize the class name when registering the plugin: `@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography" { className: wysiwyg; }`

## Using the Typography Classes

Once configured, you can apply the typography styling to your HTML: `<article class="prose lg:prose-xl">{{ markdown }}</article>`

content.text:
## Summary

**Yes, @tailwindcss/typography works with Tailwind CSS v4.**

### Installation & Configuration

**Install:**
```bash
npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography
```

**Configure (in your main CSS file, not a config file):**
```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";
```

### Key Points

- **Latest version:** 0.5.19 (May 2026 state)
- **CSS-first approach:** In v4, you register plugins directly in CSS using `@plugin` instead of a JavaScript config file
- **No tailwind.config.js needed** for basic usage—the plugin registers directly in your stylesheet
- **Custom class names:** If needed, you can customize the class name: `@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography" { className: wysiwyg; }`
- **Advanced customization:** For deep customization, you can optionally add `@config "./tailwind.config.js"` and extend the typography theme there

### Usage

Apply the `prose` class to any container:
```html
<article class="prose lg:prose-xl">
  <!-- Your content here -->
</article>
```

Available modifiers include size variants (`prose-sm`, `prose-lg`, `prose-xl`, `prose-2xl`), color themes (gray, slate, zinc, neutral, stone), dark mode support (`prose-invert`), and element-specific modifiers like `prose-headings:underline`.

### Note on Known Issues

Some users reported compatibility issues when first upgrading to v4, but ensuring you have the latest version of the plugin (0.5.19+) and properly add the `@plugin` directive in your CSS resolves these issues.

Sources:
- [Tailwind CSS v4 Blog](https://tailwindcss.com/blog/tailwindcss-v4)
- [GitHub - tailwindcss-typography](https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss-typography)
- [npm - @tailwindcss/typography](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tailwindcss/typography)
- [Setting Up Tailwind CSS v4 in an Astro Project](https://psthelyblog.github.io/blog/guides/tailwind-v4-setup/)
- [How do I configure tailwind typography in v4 Discussion](https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/discussions/15904)
